title: Mobile audio silence — sample rate mismatch (24000 vs 44100)
notes: iOS Safari audio fully working. Three fixes: (1) Safari ignores sampleRate param — fallback to default with resampling, (2) AudioContext pre-creation on user gesture, (3) client-side resampling of mic input from Safari's rate to 16kHz for Whisper.

title: Unbounded audio buffer memory growth — 30s cap added
notes: 30s audio buffer cap auto-stops listening. Claude also added running buffer counter for memory efficiency.

title: XSS in renderMarkdown — escape HTML before markdown transforms
notes:
Fixed: renderMarkdown() now calls escapeHtml() before applying markdown regex transforms.
Committed in f7aa6ab. renderMarkdown escapes HTML before markdown transforms.

title: Higgs TTS backend already scaffolded (tts_higgs.py) — wire into TTS router
notes: Claude already created tts_higgs.py with HiggsTTS class: streaming PCM via httpx, Boson API key support, fallback on timeout/error. Need to: (1) add BOSON_API_KEY to .env and systemd unit, (2) wire into tts.py TTSRouter as primary backend, (3) test latency vs Supertonic, (4) integrate _inject_control_tokens() output into Higgs input. Bead vtw tracks Phase 1.

title: Firefox DoH + Tailscale WebSocket fix — Tailscale Serve proxy on port 443
notes: Fixed: Firefox DNS-over-HTTPS can't resolve Tailscale MagicDNS names for WebSocket connections (plain fetch() uses system DNS, works fine). Solution: Tailscale Serve proxy at /voice → http://127.0.0.1:8442 (plain HTTP backend). Voice server now runs dual listeners: TLS on 8443 + plain HTTP on 8442 for proxy. Access at https://openclaw.tailf9480.ts.net/voice/ on standard port 443. Also added diagnostic logging: [connect] messages show host, protocol, wsUrl; 3s timeout detects stuck CONNECTING state.

title: Strip Higgs control tokens from text before Supertonic fallback
notes:
When TTS router falls back to Supertonic, Higgs control tokens like <|emotion:contemplation|> would be spoken literally. Need a tokenizer that strips these before sending to Supertonic.

Regex: <|[^|]+|> — matches all Higgs control tokens
Applied before Supertonic synthesis, passed through for Higgs.

Also: strip from STT transcription if somehow present (defensive).
close_reason: _strip_control_tokens implemented in TTSRouter — regex strips <|...|> tokens before Supertonic fallback

title: Higgs latency benchmark — compare cloud API vs Supertonic vs local GPU
notes:
Measure and compare TTS latency across all backends.

Benchmarks:
1. Supertonic (local, CPU): time-to-first-audio, total synthesis, sentence throughput
2. Higgs Cloud API: same metrics + network overhead
3. Higgs Local GPU (when available): same metrics, zero network latency

Test sentences:
- Short (10 words): 'Hello, what can I help you with?'
- Medium (30 words): typical voice response
- Long (60 words): extended explanation
- With control tokens: <|emotion:contemplation|>What do you think about that?

Metrics to track:
- time_to_first_audio_ms
- total_synthesis_ms
- audio_duration_ms (for real-time factor)
- network_latency_ms (cloud only)

Log to /health latency object so it's visible in the UI.

title: Higgs Audio v3 — streaming PCM client implementation
notes:
Implement the streaming PCM client for Higgs TTS API.

Boson cloud API streaming:
- POST https://api.boson.ai/v1/audio/speech
- Set stream: true, response_format: 'pcm'
- Response is raw 16-bit/24kHz/mono PCM byte stream
- No SSE framing when stream_format='audio' — just raw bytes
- Headers report sample_rate, channel count, bit depth

SGLang-Omni local serving:
- Same /v1/audio/speech endpoint
- Also supports SSE streaming with base64-encoded chunks
- stream_format='audio' + response_format='pcm' for raw bytes

Implementation:
- httpx.AsyncClient with streaming response
- Parse PCM chunks as they arrive (same format as Supertonic post-resample)
- Feed directly into existing audio_chunk pipeline (no resampling needed)
- Track time-to-first-byte for latency metrics
- Handle connection errors with fallback to Supertonic

Error handling:
- Timeout (3s default for cloud, 1s for local)
- Rate limiting (429) → fallback
- Auth errors (401) → log and fallback
- Server errors (5xx) → retry once, then fallback
close_reason: Streaming PCM client via httpx.AsyncClient with chunked response

title: TTS router — Higgs primary, Supertonic fallback
notes:
Implement a TTS router that selects the best available backend per request.

Logic:
1. Check agent config: does this agent have Higgs-specific settings (ref_audio, control tokens)?
2. If Higgs is configured and available → route to Higgs API
3. If Higgs fails (timeout >3s, error, no API key) → fall back to Supertonic
4. If offline → Supertonic only
5. Configurable: OPENCLAW_TTS_BACKEND=higgs|supertonic|auto (default: auto)

Implementation:
- tts.py: TTSRouter class that wraps multiple backends
- Each backend implements the same interface: synthesize_stream(text, voice, agent_hint) -> AsyncGenerator[bytes, None]
- Health check: /health shows active backend and fallback status
- Latency tracking: log which backend was used and how long it took

The router pattern de-risks the Higgs dependency — if the API goes down or rate-limits, Supertonic takes over seamlessly.
close_reason: TTSRouter class — Higgs primary, Supertonic fallback, configurable via OPENCLAW_TTS_BACKEND

title: Control token injection per agent — emotion, prosody, style in TTS output
notes:
Inject Higgs control tokens into TTS input based on agent personality.

Each agent gets a control token prefix in AGENT_VOICE_HINTS that shapes HOW they speak:
- Métis: <|emotion:contemplation|><|prosody:pause|> — thoughtful, probing, pauses
- Atlas: <|emotion:determination|> — direct, steady, decisive  
- Hephaestus: <|emotion:contentment|><|prosody:speed_slow|> — precise, methodical
- Clio: <|emotion:contemplation|><|prosody:speed_slow|> — measured, evidence-based
- Deepthought: <|emotion:enthusiasm|> — clear, journalistic
- Mara: <|emotion:affection|><|prosody:pause|> — warm, empathetic

Tag categories available:
- Emotion (20+): elation, amusement, enthusiasm, determination, pride, contentment, affection, relief, contemplation, confusion, surprise, awe, longing, anger, fear, disgust, bitterness, sadness, shame, helplessness
- Style: shouting, whispering, singing
- Prosody: speed_slow, speed_very_slow, pause, long_pause, pitch_high, pitch_low, expressive_high
- SFX: cough, sneeze, laughter, sigh, breathing

Implementation:
1. Add higgs_tags field to AGENT_VOICE_HINTS
2. In tts.py, prepend control tokens to input text before sending to Higgs
3. Tags are placed at start of input (positional tags like pause go inline)
4. When Supertonic is fallback, strip control tokens (they'd be ignored anyway)

This is the key differentiator — personality lives in the voice, not just the words.

title: Phase 1: Boson Cloud API integration — test latency, add HiggsTTS backend
notes:
Add Higgs as a TTS backend using the Boson cloud API (free preview).

Steps:
1. Sign up for Boson API key at boson.ai
2. Create HiggsTTS class in tts.py implementing the same interface as ChatterboxTTS
3. API endpoint: POST https://api.boson.ai/v1/audio/speech
4. Request format: {model: 'higgs-audio-v3-tts', input: text, voice: 'eleanor', response_format: 'pcm', stream: true}
5. Parse streaming PCM response (16-bit/24kHz/mono) — same format as Supertonic after resampling
6. Add OPENCLAW_TTS_HIGGS env vars: BOSON_API_KEY, HIGGS_DEFAULT_VOICE
7. Test latency: measure time-to-first-audio and total synthesis time vs Supertonic
8. Add to config.py and health endpoint

Expected latency: 200-500ms network + Boson inference time.
Risk: Rate limits during free preview, pricing TBD.
close_reason: HiggsTTS class created with Boson Cloud API streaming PCM support

title: Higgs Audio v3 TTS backend integration
notes: Epic: Higgs Audio v3 TTS integration. Sub-issues: vtw (Phase 1 cloud API), 43c (Phase 2 local GPU), 9ee (Phase 3 voice cloning), boq (control tokens), 4td (TTS router), mdu (streaming client), luu (latency benchmark), 614 (config schema), ekn (token stripping). Related: fo1 (initial research).
close_reason: Epic complete: HiggsTTS backend, streaming PCM client, TTSRouter, control token injection, config settings, health endpoint

title: Barge-in: interrupt AI TTS when user starts speaking
notes: Ready for dev. Depends on VAD endpointing (o8s). Client already has cancelAllAudio(). Server needs to: detect new speech during TTS playback via VAD, cancel remaining TTS, stop LLM streaming, start new STT. Matt wants another agent to implement.
close_reason: Pipeline runs in background task. Audio messages during playback are VAD-scanned for speech. SPEECH_START cancels pipeline, sends interrupt to client, starts fresh listen. Client: starts recording on transcript for barge-in, handles interrupt message.

title: Server-side VAD endpointing — auto-stop recording when user stops talking
notes: Ready for dev. Infrastructure in place: Silero VAD loaded, pipeline.py has sentence-level streaming, client has cancelAllAudio(). Implementation: server-side VAD detection → send stop_listening event → truncate STT → start LLM. Matt wants another agent to implement.
close_reason: Added VADEndpoint state machine (silent→speaking→stopping) with configurable hangover time. Wired into WS handler — auto-triggers pipeline on speech_end.

title: Concurrent session safety — per-request agent_hint replaces shared state
notes: Claude fixed: backend._current_agent was a race condition for concurrent sessions. Now agent_hint flows per-request: session.agent_id → pipeline → backend.chat_stream(). TTS voice switching uses session-scoped state with locks.

title: iOS Safari AudioContext compatibility — sampleRate fallback, gesture unlock, mic resampling
notes: Fixed: Safari ignores sampleRate, requires user gesture for AudioContext, and creates mic context at wrong rate. Client now resamples both input (→16kHz) and output (→context rate). Global unlock on first touch/mousedown.

title: Configurable voice model — use faster model for voice without changing agent default
notes: Implemented: OPENCLAW_VOICE_MODEL env var. Set to 'nvidia/deepseek-ai/deepseek-v4-flash' for ~3x faster first-token latency. Gateway still routes through agent for persona/memory.

title: Runtime pip install in tts.py — removed
notes: Fixed: replaced with ImportError warning to add to requirements.txt.

title: CORS allow_credentials=True with allow_origins=['*'] — fixed to False
notes: Fixed: allow_credentials=False. Credentials not needed for WebSocket voice interface.

title: Barge-in support: interrupt AI response when user starts speaking
notes: Superseded by o8s (VAD endpointing) and gr5 (barge-in).

title: Server-side VAD endpointing for continuous mode
notes: Superseded by openclaw-voice-o8s which covers both VAD endpointing and barge-in.

title: Upgrade STT from base to small model for better accuracy
notes: STT upgraded from base to small model. systemd env var updated.

title: HTTPS redirect — :8443 direct access should redirect to /voice on port 443
notes: Users who bookmark :8443 will keep hitting the Firefox DoH bug. Add a redirect on the TLS listener (8443): if User-Agent is a browser, redirect to https://openclaw.tailf9480.ts.net/voice/. Also add a notice page explaining the Tailscale+Firefox issue.

title: Dual-process model loading — 2x memory for STT/TTS
notes: Fixed: replaced multiprocessing.Process with asyncio.gather of two uvicorn.Server instances. Both TLS (8443) and plain HTTP (8442) listeners run in the same process, sharing one model load. Memory: 1.7GB vs 2.2GB+ before.

title: VAD not loading — torchaudio ABI3 missing
notes: Journal shows: 'VAD not available: Could not load this library: torchaudio/lib/_torchaudio.abi3.so'. Silero VAD model downloads but torchaudio shared library fails to load. VAD shows as 'loaded' in /health but may not actually be functional. Fix: pin compatible torchaudio version or install from conda. Affects server-side VAD endpointing (o8s) and barge-in (gr5).

title: Agent voice config schema — ref_audio, control tokens, preset voice per agent
notes:
Define the configuration schema for per-agent voice settings.

Current AGENT_VOICE_HINTS (text-based system prompts for voice personality):
- metis: 'curious, warm, probing — thinking companion'
- atlas: 'direct, steady, decisive — reliable coordinator'
- etc.

Extended schema for Higgs:
AGENT_VOICE_CONFIG = {
  'metis': {
    'higgs_voice': 'eleanor',           # Preset voice
    'higgs_ref_audio': 'voices/metis.wav',  # Zero-shot clone reference
    'higgs_ref_text': 'Hello, I am Métis...',  # Transcript of reference
    'higgs_tags': '<|emotion:contemplation|><|prosody:pause|>',  # Control tokens
    'supertonic_voice': 'F2',           # Fallback for Supertonic
  },
  ...
}

Priority: ref_audio > preset voice > Supertonic fallback
Control tokens prepended to input text when Higgs is active, stripped when Supertonic is active.
close_reason: AGENT_VOICE_CONFIG dict with hint, higgs_voice, higgs_tags, supertonic_voice per agent. Replaces old AGENT_VOICE_HINTS flat dict. Backend and TTS router use it.

title: Phase 3: Agent voice cloning with ref_audio
notes:
Give each agent a unique voice using Higgs zero-shot voice cloning.

Steps:
1. Record 5-10 second reference clips for each agent (Métis, Atlas, Hephaestus, Clio, Deepthought, Mara)
2. Store as voices/metis.wav, voices/atlas.wav, etc.
3. Create ref_text transcripts for each clip
4. In AGENT_VOICE_HINTS, add ref_audio and ref_text fields per agent
5. When synthesizing for agent X, pass ref_audio and ref_text to Higgs API
6. Higgs clones the voice from the reference — each agent sounds distinct
7. Fallback: if ref_audio not available, use preset voice (eleanor, jake, etc.)

Higgs API format:
  ref_audio: URL, data URI, or base64-encoded audio
  ref_text: transcript of reference audio (improves quality)

Alternative: Create reusable custom voices via Boson API (if available) to avoid sending ref_audio every request.

title: Phase 2: Local GPU inference via SGLang-Omni
notes:
Run Higgs Audio v3 locally on a GPU for zero-latency TTS.

Steps:
1. Add a GPU to Proxmox (RTX 3060 12GB ~50, or RTX 4060 8GB ~00)
2. Passthrough GPU to the OpenClaw Voice VM
3. Install sglang-omni: pip install sglang-omni
4. Download model: hf download bosonai/higgs-audio-v3-tts-4b (~9.3GB BF16)
5. Serve locally: sgl-omni serve --model-path bosonai/higgs-audio-v3-tts-4b --port 8000
6. Same OpenAI-compatible API as cloud, zero network latency
7. INT4 quantization (~3GB VRAM) possible for smaller GPUs
8. Update OPENCLAW_TTS_HIGGS_URL env var to point to localhost:8000

Alternative: Run on separate GPU server on Tailscale network.

Context length: 8,192 tokens. For single-user voice, a single concurrent request at a time.
Expected latency: <100ms first audio.

title: Higgs Audio v3 TTS as TTS backend — controllable agent voices
notes: Research complete. Superseded by epic openclaw-voice-ben and Phase 1 openclaw-voice-vtw.

title: WebRTC transport for lower-latency audio
notes: Current WebSocket transport has ~200-500ms overhead per chunk. WebRTC would reduce this to ~50ms with built-in audio codecs (Opus). Claude already scaffolded webrtc.py. Needs: (1) WebRTC signaling via existing WebSocket, (2) Opus encoding on server, (3) Client WebRTC setup. Estimated 30-50% latency reduction for first audio. Lower priority since current latency is acceptable for voice.

title: OpenClaw gateway reconnection — resume conversation across server restarts
notes: If oc-voice restarts, WebSocket drops and conversation context is lost. OpenClaw gateway has the full conversation history. On reconnect: (1) client sends session_id or agent name, (2) server fetches recent history from gateway to seed the context, (3) conversation picks up where it left off. The gateway already owns memory — we just need to query it.
close_reason: chat_stream accepts reconnect flag. When True, gateway gets context resumption message. Client stores session in localStorage, sends reconnect flag on reconnection.

title: Streaming latency optimization — speculative TTS, sentence-level parallelism
notes: Current ~12s total (STT=2s, LLM=8s, first_audio=5s). Targets: connection pooling for gateway, speculative TTS, STT streaming. Matt wants another agent to implement.

title: Pitch/tone post-processing for agent voice distinctiveness
notes: Superseded by openclaw-voice-boq (Higgs control tokens provide emotion/prosody/style per agent, which is far more effective than pitch shifting).

title: Voice cloning from audio samples via Supertonic get_voice_style_from_path()
notes: Superseded by openclaw-voice-9ee (Phase 3 voice cloning via Higgs ref_audio, which is superior to Supertonic get_voice_style_from_path()).

title: Client-side audio playback robustness — error recovery, retry, debug logging
notes: iOS AudioContext interrupted state recovery, retry on WebSocket disconnect during playback, console.warn on sample rate mismatch. Matt wants another agent to implement.

notes: Claude shipped: regex for sentence splitting pre-compiled, float32 utility in audio_utils.py, separate thread executors for STT/TTS, parallel model loading on startup, TTS errors now raise instead of silent fail.

notes: Browser was caching stale HTML after deploys, causing stale JS to run (broken sample rate, old features). Added Cache-Control: no-cache, no-store, must-revalidate.

notes: Implemented: AGENT_VOICE_HINTS maps each agent to a personality prompt. Métis=curious/probing, Atlas=direct/decisive, Hephaestus=precise/methodical, Clio=thoughtful/evidence-based, Deepthought=journalistic, Mara=warm/empathetic. Hints sent as system message alongside user message; gateway provides full persona.

notes: Fixed by Claude: per-request agent_hint parameter replaces shared backend._current_agent state. TTS voice switching uses session-scoped state with locks. History and rate-limiting also use per-session locks.

notes: Implemented: ChatterboxTTS.AGENT_VOICE_MAP class attribute, dynamic voice switching on agent selection, health endpoint reports map.

title: Conversation history with context window management
notes: OpenClaw gateway mode sends only voice hint + user message. No history duplication. Gateway owns conversation memory for cross-channel continuity.

notes: Superseded by openclaw-voice-bi6 (more detailed WebRTC transport issue).

title: Favicon for voice UI — stop the 404s
notes: Added favicon.svg (purple gradient voice icon). Served at /favicon.svg and /favicon.ico. No more 404s.

title: TTS response caching — avoid redundant synthesis
notes: If same text is synthesized twice, return cached audio instead of re-running TTS. Saves compute especially for repeated phrases. Redis or file-based cache with TTL. Article on production TTS (Piper→Polly) highlights this as essential for multi-user scenarios. Also: distributed locks to prevent duplicate synthesis when multiple users request same text simultaneously.
close_reason: TTSCache LRU with TTL in TTSRouter — caches by (text, voice) key, max 128 entries, 300s TTL

title: Docker containerization — production-ready deployment
notes: Dockerfile exists but needs: (1) Multi-stage build (build deps vs runtime), (2) GPU passthrough for Supertonic CUDA, (3) Health check endpoint (already /health), (4) Volume mounts for models and voices, (5) Environment variable configuration, (6) Non-root user. Current deployment is systemd unit on bare metal which works but isn't portable.
close_reason: Multi-stage Dockerfile, non-root user, volume mounts for models/voices, NVIDIA GPU support, HEALTHCHECK
